mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that drives corporate revenue expectations upward, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that boosts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during recovery periods, or the predictive coefficient turns negative in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that supports corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 2.5% annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% YoY, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that elevates earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, with interaction term coefficients statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing contraction that triggers immediate profit downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.
